note: Sonnet B's single false-refusal in run-2 (within the <=10% bar: 1/50=0.02). Recorded per Fable for the O10 memo.

sonnet_b_false_refusal.reason: Object/scope roughly line up (fuel expense for the fleet, both ranked as second-largest operating cost after labor). But mechanism is not confirmed as identical: Side A only says the fuel burned "cost more" -- a generic total-cost statement that could stem from higher price, higher volume burned (more flying), or both. Side B specifically attributes the increase to "the reported price... increased," i.e., a price-per-unit driver. Side B carries a price-specific qualifier that Side A's quote lacks, so the two are not confirmed to be measuring the same causal mechanism -- this is an unresolved qualifier asymmetry, not symmetric silence, so per the default-DIFFERENT rule this fails the MECHANISM check.
